---
title: "MENA-PERC"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
---

## MENA-PERC

**MENA Political Elite Research Consortium (MENA-PERC)** is a research project that studies the structure, behavior, and transformation of political elites across the Middle East and North Africa. The project combines large-scale data collection with computational text analysis to examine how elite coalitions evolve, how power is distributed within regimes, and how these dynamics shape processes of regime stability, crisis, and authoritarian transformation.

---

## 🔍 About

MENA-PERC focuses on the systematic analysis of political elites using original datasets on appointments, careers, and legislative behavior. A central goal of the project is to understand how intra-elite relations—such as cooperation, competition, and exclusion—affect political outcomes, including democratization, authoritarian consolidation, and conflict.

The project integrates:
- Large-scale data on political appointments and careers  
- Legislative speech corpora and text-as-data approaches  
- Theoretical frameworks on elite power-sharing and regime dynamics  

---

## 📂 Resources

- [Data](./data/)  
  Datasets on political elites, appointments, and legislative behavior  

- [Code](./code/)  
  Data processing pipelines, text analysis scripts, and replication material  

- [Papers](./papers/)  
  Publications and working papers related to MENA-PERC  

- [Outputs](./outputs/)  
  Figures, dashboards, and derived data products  

---

## 📊 Key Outputs

- Elite-level datasets covering multiple MENA countries  
- Text-based measures of ideological positioning and regime support  
- Empirical analyses of elite behavior during regime crises  

(links to specific outputs can be added here)

---

## 👥 Team

- Kevin Koehler — Scuola Superiore Sant’Anna  
- (add collaborators here)

---

## 📬 Contact

For questions, collaborations, or data access requests, please contact:  
**Kevin Koehler**  
Scuola Superiore Sant’Anna
